messages: Having on-site or in-unit laundry would make things a lot easier mid-trip.
No visa concerns on our side—our passports are current well beyond the trip dates.
I don't want to overbook each day; planning for roughly two main activities per day feels about right for our energy levels.
Given these preferences and constraints, could you start outlining realistic options, or is there any single detail you still need?
Before you put together anything formal, can you list what deliverables you would produce so I know what to expect to share with my partner?
I'm hoping for a day-by-day itinerary, suggested flight options, two lodging choices per stop (one more central and one quieter), and local transport recommendations—can you prepare all of that?
Please also include a short note each day about how to keep the child on a comfortable schedule—nap windows and easy meal options would be great.
When you recommend travel between stops, prioritize options that avoid overnight legs and keep luggage handling to a minimum.
For budgeting, give a clear breakdown for flights, lodging, local transit, food, and a small contingency, without assuming we'll want luxury.
I don't need full bookings now, but two concrete flight options each way with estimated prices would help in decision-making.
For places to stay, favor walkable neighborhoods that are quieter in the evenings and close to basic stores and small parks.
Make sure intercity travel choices aren't too long in single stretches so daily rhythms and naps aren't regularly disrupted.
Include a short packing checklist focused on space-saving and child-related essentials that speed up transitions (e.g., quick meals, quick changes).
Also provide two light 'plan B' alternatives per day in case we need to stay local for rest or unexpected delays.
At the end, rate each full option (itinerary + lodging + flights) for ease of travel with a small child and for cost-effectiveness, and flag any choices likely to push us beyond our cost cap.
When you're finished, compile everything into a concise, shareable brief I can forward to my partner for quick approval.
